_meta.license: Copyright Oliver P. John (BFI originally John, Donahue, & Kentle, 1991; John & Srivastava, 1999). Not public domain, but made freely available by the Berkeley Personality Lab for non-commercial research, teaching, and personal use; commercial use requires permission.
_meta.attribution: John, O. P., & Srivastava, S. (1999). The Big Five trait taxonomy: History, measurement, and theoretical perspectives. In L. A. Pervin & O. P. John (Eds.), Handbook of Personality: Theory and Research (2nd ed., pp. 102-138). New York: Guilford Press. Instrument originally: John, O. P., Donahue, E. M., & Kentle, R. L. (1991). The Big Five Inventory - Versions 4a and 54. Berkeley: University of California, Berkeley, Institute of Personality and Social Research. Distributed by the Berkeley Personality Lab.
_meta.scoring_notes: 44 items, each rated 1 (Disagree strongly) to 5 (Agree strongly), all sharing the stem "I see myself as someone who ...". Item texts here are stored as the phrase that follows that stem, in the instrument's lowercase form (e.g., item 1 reads "I see myself as someone who is talkative"). Reverse-keyed items (keyed=minus) are recoded as 6 minus the raw response, then each scale is the mean of its items. Canonical scoring key (R = reverse): Extraversion 1, 6R, 11, 16, 21R, 26, 31R, 36 (8 items); Agreeableness 2R, 7, 12R, 17, 22, 27R, 32, 37R, 42 (9 items); Conscientiousness 3, 8R, 13, 18R, 23R, 28, 33, 38, 43R (9 items); Neuroticism 4, 9R, 14, 19, 24R, 29, 34R, 39 (8 items); Openness 5, 10, 15, 20, 25, 30, 35R, 40, 41R, 44 (10 items). Total 44. The BFI-44 has no facet subscales (facets are a feature of the later BFI-2), so facet is left blank. Keying and item-to-factor mapping were taken verbatim from the Wisconsin ARC / Berkeley scoring key.
_meta.cautions: Item text was fetched verbatim from db.arabpsychology.com (a psychological-scales reproduction of the BFI-44); its wording matches the canonical John & Srivastava (1999) instrument and was independently corroborated by search snippets for items 28 ("perseveres until the task is finished"), 35 ("prefers work that is routine"), and 44 ("is sophisticated in art, music, or literature"). The original Berkeley Personality Lab (ocf.berkeley.edu/~johnlab) now gates the questionnaire .doc behind a post-survey download and its page could not be scraped directly; the ResearchGate PDF returned HTTP 403. The scoring/keying was confirmed from the University of Wisconsin ARC self-report page (arc.psych.wisc.edu), which reproduces the standard Berkeley key. Source list-formatting capitalized each item's first letter (e.g., "Is talkative"); these were normalized to the instrument's lowercase form ("is talkative") to read correctly after the shared stem - a formatting normalization only, no wording changed. This is the BFI-44 (John & Srivastava, 1999), distinct from the newer 60-item BFI-2 and the short BFI-10/BFI-S versions. License: copyrighted by Oliver P. John but freely usable for non-commercial research/teaching/personal use, so safe for this stage-1 non-commercial corpus; commercial use would require permission from the copyright holder.

verification.summary: adversarially fact-checked by a second agent vs primary sources (deepinme wide-collection workflow, 2026-07)

items.text: tends to find fault with others
is original, comes up with new ideas
is helpful and unselfish with others
is curious about many different things
is emotionally stable, not easily upset
perseveres until the task is finished
is considerate and kind to almost everyone
makes plans and follows through with them
likes to reflect, play with ideas
is sophisticated in art, music, or literature
